name: Control_Mech

infobox.Systems: Control Shot(Science)EffectTemporarily control a unit, moving it a short distance. (Starts with 2-tile distance)Upgrade I+1 Move ()Targeted unit can more one extra tile.Upgrade II+1 Move ()Targeted unit can more one extra tile.
infobox.Special: Flying: Flying units can move over any terrain tile.

sections.Team: The mech is available as part of theMist Eaterssquad.
sections.Strategy:
The Control Mech can somewhat freely move enemies around with Control Shot, which is well-suited for making them attack other Vek. However, much like theSwap Mech, it relies on Reactor Cores to scale better as the run continues; although the Control Mech arguably starts stronger, due to having more range to reposition enemies and being able to move them in less limited angles.
To get the most out of Control Shot, find a high damage Vek — such asLeapersand Alphas — earlier in the turn Attack Order, and then move it so it aims at a Vek attacking later. This effectively neutralizes two threats in one action. Alternatively, Vek can be moved into position to block a projectile attack from an enemy like aFirefly, which accomplishes the same objective. Vek can also be moved into environmental effects that will kill them, onto spawns to block them, into Fire tiles to damage them each turn, into Smoke to cancel their attacks, or into position to set up collisions for your other Mechs' pushing weapons.
Control Shot can also move other allied units that can move regularly — including Mechs, Sentient Weapons, and allied Tanks — before or after their turns action has been spent. This can be situationally helpful for moving webbed allies to safety or letting these units move farther.
However, note that the controlled movement is limited both by the Control Shot's range, and by the targets natural movement limitations:
  - Ground-based Vek and ally units can't be controlled to move over water (and relatedly can't be dropped directly into water), unless they areMassive(for example,Vek Leaders).
  - Ground-based units can't move through terrain blocked by enemy units, or terrain obstacles such as Mountains.
  - Conversely, units that can fly, leap or burrow under such obstacles can be controlled more freely.
  - Control Shot can't move enemies or allies who have been frozen or have no movement points; the latter which includes theEarth Mover,Blobs,Spiderling Eggsand Boulders.
Much like with the Mechs own movement points, the first+1 Rangeupgrade costing 1 Reactor Core — for a total of 3 controlled movement of Vek — gives significantly more options and is a priority upgrade. The second +1 Range upgrade — for a total of 4 Range when both are powered — is also quite useful, but delivers less value per core.
Similarly to theExchange Mech, the Control Mech also works quite well in manyCustom Squadcombinations. One notable Mech is theGravity Mech, whoseVek Hormonespassive allows Control Shot's utility to force enemies into destroying each other more easily.
As with other Science class Mechs,Kazaaakplethoffers a useful damaging push for when the control effect is not needed. This orHarold Schmidt's multi-directional push can be especially useful for the Mist Eaters, who do not otherwise begin with a pushing weapon.
